_comment: Premium per-spot content: spearing rating, seasonal fish abundance, hunting tips, 14-day AI visibility prediction. Paywalled on the frontend.

coronado.summary: Sand-bottom beach break south of the bay mouth. Low-visibility, low-structure. Okay for surfperch and occasional halibut drifts.
huntingTips: Halibut lie on sand transitions — look for color changes at 8–15ft.
Approach low and slow, shoot behind the gill plate before they spook.
Best window is an hour after dawn on a flooding tide.
coronado.prediction14Day: Expect moderate 10–14ft visibility to hold this week with a drop Friday as a short-period swell arrives. Chlorophyll trending slightly up.

imperial-beach.summary: Often impacted by Tijuana River plume — check closures. Poor clarity most of the year.
huntingTips: Always check TJ River plume and county health advisories before entering.
Stick to the north end closer to the pier for cleaner water.
Halibut drifts on the outside sand at 10–15ft after a north swell.
imperial-beach.prediction14Day: Visibility likely to stay 8–12ft with possible closures after rain events. Avoid following any south swell.

point-loma.summary: Classic San Diego kelp spearing. Deep reef, big fish, real structure. The stand-out spot in the county for size.
huntingTips: White seabass: dawn patrol at the edge of kelp stringers. Listen for croaks under the canopy.
Yellowtail: look for bait balls and diving birds on the outside kelp lines in summer.
Set up ambush behind kelp — don't chase. Breathe up long, stay flat on the bottom.
Lingcod: bottom at 50–70ft in the rock piles. Aim for the head, they don't move.
point-loma.prediction14Day: Strong clarity trend continuing 22–28ft through mid-week. Possible dip Friday–Saturday with SW swell. Excellent white seabass window opening next week.

sunset-cliffs.summary: Accessible reef structure right off the cliffs. Solid bass and sheephead spot with occasional bigger visitors.
huntingTips: Time your entry on a low slack — the cliff stairs are brutal on a rising tide.
Work the kelp edges in 15–20ft for calico bass ambush shots.
Sheephead hang near rock piles — males guard territory, approach from above.
sunset-cliffs.prediction14Day: Holding 18–24ft through the weekend. Chlorophyll low and dropping. Strong window Wednesday–Friday.

ocean-beach.summary: Sandy beach break with OB Pier. Better for halibut drifts than reef fish.
huntingTips: Drift parallel to the pier pilings for halibut ambushes.
Morning glass-off windows are rare but golden.
Watch for riptides around the jetty.
ocean-beach.prediction14Day: Typical 12–16ft through the period. Short-period swell midweek will knock clarity down temporarily.

mission-beach.summary: Long stretch of sand. Halibut on the drifts, little reef structure.
huntingTips: Drift the sand in 10–15ft looking for halibut outlines.
Corbina cruise the inside shore break in summer — stalk them on the surface.
Avoid weekend afternoons due to swim traffic.
mission-beach.prediction14Day: Expect 12–16ft with dips after weekend surf events.

mission-bay.summary: Protected bay — flat water, low visibility. Spearfishing is generally not the move here. Good for halibut drifts in the channels.
huntingTips: Work channel edges on incoming tide.
Visibility rarely exceeds 10ft — use feel and close-range shots.
Check bay regulations — not all areas open to spearfishing.
mission-bay.prediction14Day: 6–10ft consistently. Bay clarity is tide-driven, not swell-driven.

pacific-beach.summary: Crescent Bay sand with Crystal Pier. Halibut spot.
huntingTips: Drift between Crystal Pier and the Tourmaline jetty.
Target sand transition lines in 10–20ft.

bird-rock.summary: Small reef headland between PB and La Jolla. Decent calico and sheephead spot with tricky entries.
huntingTips: Entry at the north access point on a low tide.
Work the reef edges in 15–20ft.
Stay alert to surge — rocks come up fast.

windansea.summary: Famous surf spot with reef structure. Good bass and sheephead spot when swell allows.
huntingTips: Only dive here when the surf is small — Windansea gets heavy fast.
Work the south reef for calicos in 15–25ft.
Avoid surf crowd zones — respect the lineup.
windansea.prediction14Day: 18–22ft trending up. Small incoming swell could push clarity higher.

la-jolla-cove.summary: La Jolla Ecological Reserve — NO SPEARFISHING INSIDE THE RESERVE. Marked as 'scenic' for divers and swimmers. For adjacent legal zones, move south toward Marine Room.
huntingTips: Know the reserve boundaries — enforcement is serious here.
Legal spearfishing begins outside the reserve line south of Marine Room.
Use this spot for scouting and training freedives, not for shots.
la-jolla-cove.prediction14Day: 25–30ft continuing through week. Top clarity window of the whole coast.

underwater-park.summary: La Jolla Underwater Park & Ecological Reserve — NO SPEARFISHING. Legendary kelp forest visibility. Photograph only, train here.
huntingTips: This is a no-take reserve. Use it for free diving practice and scouting.
Nearby Point Loma and south La Jolla Shores sand channels are legal.
Respect the rules — this reserve is why San Diego has so much fish density.
underwater-park.prediction14Day: Peak clarity 28–34ft forecast through mid-week. Likely cleanest water in the region.

torrey-pines.summary: Long beach at the base of the cliffs. Sandy with occasional rock outcrops. Good for halibut drifts with sporadic reef fish.
huntingTips: Long walks down the beach reward you with empty water.
Target sand-to-rock transitions for halibut.
Watch tide — access gets cut off on a big high.

del-mar.summary: Beach break with occasional cobble. Mostly halibut and surfperch. 15th Street reef holds some calicos.
huntingTips: Work 15th Street reef zones for scattered structure.
Sand drifts produce halibut in 10–18ft.
del-mar.prediction14Day: 14–18ft expected, with dip after weekend swell.

solana-beach.summary: Small reef pockets under the bluffs. Fletcher Cove entry. Decent calico and sheephead spot.
huntingTips: Enter at Fletcher Cove and swim north along the reef edges.
Calicos ambush from reef holes in 15–25ft.
Watch for swell — rocks come up quickly.

encinitas.summary: Swami's and Moonlight reef zones offer real structure. Good bass, sheephead, and rare white seabass encounters.
huntingTips: Swami's reef zones in 20–30ft hold the larger calicos.
Dawn patrols produce the best chance at a visiting white seabass.
Moonlight Beach entry is the easiest.

carlsbad.summary: Mostly sand with small reef sections near Terramar. Halibut spot with occasional calicos.
huntingTips: Terramar reef in 15–20ft holds the few calicos in the area.
Power plant outfall zone is closed to spearfishing.
Sandy drifts produce halibut throughout the year.

seal-beach.summary: Bay-influenced flat sand. Very low visibility, limited spearing value.
huntingTips: River mouth runoff keeps clarity low — pick your days.
Halibut drifts on the outside sand bars.

huntington-beach.summary: Long sand beach with the pier. Halibut drifts and surfperch.
huntingTips: Work the pier pilings for halibut ambushes.
North of the pier cleans up faster on a south swell.

newport-beach.summary: Corona del Mar jetty and the Newport reef zones offer legitimate structure.
huntingTips: Work the jetty edges in 15–25ft.
Sheephead hold tight to the rocks — approach from above.

corona-del-mar.summary: Protected cove with solid reef. Marine protected area boundaries must be watched carefully.
huntingTips: Outside the MPA line — check CDFW boundary map before you shoot.
Big calicos in the kelp edges 20–30ft.
Sheephead patrol rock piles — look for the big black-heads.

crystal-cove.summary: Beautiful reef structure. Portions are MPA — know the boundaries.
huntingTips: Legal zone is outside the state MPA — verify before every dive.
Kelp stringers hold yellowtail in summer.
Work reef holes for calicos in 20–30ft.
crystal-cove.prediction14Day: 20–24ft trending up with clean chlorophyll signal.

laguna-beach.summary: Entire Laguna coast is MPA (no take) except for narrow zones. Verify before you dive — or use adjacent Salt Creek/Aliso. For viewing, it's unbeatable.
huntingTips: Most of Laguna's reefs are inside the Laguna Beach State Marine Reserve — zero take.
Legal spearfishing zones require CDFW map verification.
Best used for training, scouting, and freediving practice.
laguna-beach.prediction14Day: 22–28ft expected through the week — top clarity in OC.

salt-creek.summary: Reef structure below the bluffs. Good calico and sheephead spot with sporadic bigger fish.
huntingTips: Stair-down entry is the easy part — long swim to the reef.
Outside reef sections in 25–35ft hold the larger fish.
Yellowtail pass through the outside kelp in summer afternoons.

dana-point.summary: Headland with real reef structure outside the harbor. Big calico potential.
huntingTips: Boat dive for the outside reefs — shore entry is limited.
Yellowtail move through the kelp line in summer at 25–40ft.
Calicos here grow big — be patient at reef holes.
dana-point.prediction14Day: 18–22ft trending up with small clean swell.

san-clemente.summary: Beach break with reef pockets. T-Street and Trestles zones offer structure. San Mateo MPA nearby.
huntingTips: Know the San Mateo/San Onofre MPA boundaries.
Reef pockets outside T-Street hold calicos in 15–25ft.

long-beach.summary: Breakwater-protected bay water. Very low visibility. Not a spearfishing destination.
huntingTips: Visibility usually 5–10ft — close-range only.
Check harbor regulations carefully.

redondo-beach.summary: Redondo Canyon drops steep right off the pier — real structure, big fish potential for LA County.
huntingTips: The canyon edge holds big fish — work 30–50ft depth drop-offs.
White seabass move along the sand channels in summer mornings.
Watch pier fishermen — keep distance and clear of lines.

hermosa-beach.summary: Sand beach with the Hermosa Pier. Halibut and surfperch spot.
huntingTips: Drift parallel to pier pilings for halibut.
Early morning glass-offs are rare but productive.

manhattan-beach.summary: Sand beach with pier. Halibut spot.
huntingTips: Work pier pilings and sandbars for halibut.
Avoid weekend surfer crowds.

venice-beach.summary: Santa Monica Bay sand. High chlorophyll, low visibility most days.
huntingTips: Choose days after extended periods without runoff.
Sand drifts for halibut are the primary play.

santa-monica.summary: Bay sand, high chlorophyll influence, pier crowds. Limited spearing value.
huntingTips: Low viability — pick days carefully.
Pier fishing zones restricted — keep clear.

malibu.summary: Outside Santa Monica Bay influence. Real reef, real kelp, real fish. LA County's premier spearing coast.
huntingTips: Multiple reef zones from County Line to Paradise Cove — pick by swell direction.
White seabass patrol the outside kelp edges at dawn in spring/summer.
Yellowtail push in on warm water events — watch sea surface temps.
Sheephead males hit 20+ pounds on the outside reefs.

point-dume.summary: LA County's cleanest water spot. Reef juts out into open ocean — bigger fish, higher visibility.
huntingTips: Boat access preferred — shore entries are long and rough.
Outside the point in 40–70ft for big white seabass and yellowtail.
Watch surge and current — conditions change fast on the tip.

zuma-beach.summary: Long sand beach with reef pockets on the ends. Halibut and occasional bass.
huntingTips: Work the ends of Zuma where reef meets sand.
Middle of the beach is pure sand — halibut drifts only.

el-matador.summary: Sea stacks and reef zones under dramatic cliffs. Solid calico and sheephead structure.
huntingTips: Entry stairs get slippery — watch the rocks at low tide.
Work around the sea stacks in 15–25ft.
Surge picks up fast — pick low-swell days.

casino-point.summary: Casino Point Dive Park is a NO-TAKE reserve. Legendary visibility — use for training, free diving, scouting. Outside the park is legal.
huntingTips: Inside the park: zero take. Outside the orange markers: legal.
Train your freedives here — the clarity is unmatched on the mainland side.
Move to Descanso or Pebbly for legal spearing with similar structure.

descanso-beach.summary: Just outside Casino Point. Legal spearfishing with similar visibility. Kelp and reef structure.
huntingTips: Verify you're outside the Casino Point park boundary before every shot.
Kelp edges hold calicos and sheephead in 20–30ft.
Yellowtail cruise through in summer afternoons.

avalon-bay.summary: Protected bay with heavy boat traffic. Most of Avalon Bay is restricted — know the rules.
huntingTips: Heavy boat traffic — stay visible with a dive flag.
Most shore zones near the casino are restricted for spearing.

lovers-cove.summary: Lover's Cove is a NO-TAKE State Marine Conservation Area. Glass bottom boat / snorkel only zone.
huntingTips: This is a protected SMCA — zero take.
Use for snorkel scouting only. Legal spearing zones require moving well outside the cove.

pebbly-beach.summary: Less crowded option south of Avalon. Solid reef and kelp, fewer boats than Descanso.
huntingTips: Shore access is rough — most people boat in.
Outside kelp stringers for yellowtail in summer.
Calicos hold tight to reef holes in 25–35ft.

two-harbors.summary: Less pressured than Avalon. Real fish density. Outer coves produce yellowtail and white seabass all summer.
huntingTips: Boat access — Blue Cavern Point and Ship Rock are the classic spots.
Yellowtail patrol the outside kelp from May through October.
White seabass on dawn patrols along the sand channels.
Drift dive the outside edges — the fish come to you.
two-harbors.prediction14Day: 45–55ft expected through the week — peak Catalina clarity.
